what: what is a call stack in javascript
why: why does recursion cause stack overflow
why does javascript execute one function at a time
why does js know which function runs next
how: how does js know which function runs next
how are functions executed in order
how does the call stack track functions
output: predict function execution order using call stack
what happens when call stack is full

1_beginner_explanation.summary: The Call Stack is JavaScript's mechanism for tracking which function is currently running and which functions are waiting to resume. It works like a stack of plates — when a function is called it is pushed on top; when it returns it is popped off, and control returns to the function below.
1_beginner_explanation.analogy: Think of the Call Stack like a stack of sticky notes on a desk. When your boss gives you a task (function call), you write it on a note and put it on top. If that task requires you to ask a colleague (nested function call), you put a new note on top of that. You always work on the top note first. When you finish a task, you remove that note and continue the one underneath.

2_internal_mechanism.execution_flow: When the JavaScript engine starts executing a script, it pushes a Global Execution Context onto the Call Stack. Every function call creates a new Function Execution Context that is pushed on top. The engine always executes the context at the top of the stack. When a function returns (explicitly or implicitly), its context is popped. This continues until the Call Stack is empty and the program finishes.
2_internal_mechanism.memory: Each stack frame stores local variables, arguments, and a return address. Stack memory is limited by the runtime host (browsers typically allow ~10,000–15,000 frames). Exceeding this limit throws a RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded (commonly called a stack overflow).

3_real_world_example.use_case: Reading browser DevTools stack traces to pinpoint exactly which chain of function calls led to an error.

4_common_confusion.trap: Confusing the Call Stack with the Event Loop. The Call Stack runs synchronous code in order. The Event Loop only queues asynchronous callbacks when the Call Stack is completely empty.
4_common_confusion.fix: Remember: the Call Stack executes right now. The Event Loop schedules what runs next after the stack is clear. They are two separate mechanisms.

6_edge_case.quirk: Infinite recursion does not freeze the browser immediately. It grows the call stack silently until the limit is hit, then throws a RangeError. Each recursive call consumes a full stack frame even if the function body is tiny.
